---
title: "Package spotlight - SHAR"
description: |
  Here I use the SHAR R package to find which habitat types the Nankeen Kestrel is associated with around greater Adelaide. 
date: ""

Understanding wildlife habitat associations is a critical part of effective conservation management. In this post, I'll walk you through an R package, Species Habitat Associations in R (SHAR), that quantifies these relationships. The package website can be found [here](https://r-spatialecology.github.io/shar/index.html).

First up, lets load the packages and data required for the analysis. We need species observation data, which I'll be grabbing from the Atlas of Living Australia (ALA) for the Nankeen Kestrel, and environmental data, which I'll be sourcing from the luna R package in the form of land cover data.


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
library(shar) #Species Habitat Associations in R
library(tidyverse) #tidy data/wrangling
library(auk) #eBird data wrangling
library(sf) #spatial data operations
library(terra) #raster data operations
library(tidyterra) #raster plotting
library(scales) #pretty scales
library(paletteer) #colours for plotting
library(arrow) #read/write data
library(galah) #Nankeen Kestrel observations
library(luna) #package to source land cover data

# Kestrel Data
#kestrel_dat <- read_parquet()
```
:::


Now, lets grab the Nankeen Kestrel observations from ALA. This bird holds a special place in my heart, as it was initially the focus of my PhD. We will source all observations from within the greater adelaide area.


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
#configure package
#galah_config(email = "your-email-here")
```
:::
